title: Valve assembly and dispenser
abstract: The present invention provides a valve assembly for a dispenser for dispensing fluids comprising: a septum (120)of elastomeric material configured for fluid-tight engagement within a dispensing opening of a container. In a first configuration the septum prevents fluid from being dispensed. The septum comprises at least one slit traversing a centre region thereof to form at least two petaline portions in contiguous relationship along said slit that are configured to elastically distend responsive to insertion of an end of a fluid transfer conduit (150) or syringe through the slit into a second configuration. In the second configuration the petaline portions are opened to permit the fluid transfer conduit to extend therethrough. In this second configuration the petaline portions resiliently engage against one or more walls of said fluid transfer conduit and are further configured to self-close by returning to said first configuration responsive to withdrawal of said fluid transfer conduit.
claims:
Claims A valve assembly for a dispenser for dispensing fluids comprising: a septum of elastomeric material configured for fluid-tight engagement within a dispensing opening of a container, said septum in a first configuration to prevent fluid from being dispensed comprising at least one slit traversing a centre region thereof to form at least two petaline portions in contiguous relationship along said slit and configured to elastically distend responsive to insertion of a fluid transfer conduit through the slit into a second configuration opening said at least two petaline portions that permit said fluid transfer conduit to extend therethrough and in which said second configuration said petaline portions are configured to resiliently engage against one or more walls of said fluid transfer conduit and are further configured to self-close by returning to said first configuration responsive to withdrawal of said fluid transfer conduit. A valve assembly according to claim 1, wherein the septum if formed from a resilient polymeric material. A valve assembly according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the petaline portions are configured, so that in use, withdrawal of the fluid transfer conduit causes the petaline portions to wipe the walls of the fluid transfer conduit to remove residual material therefrom. A valve assembly according to any of claims 1 to 3, wherein the septum forms part of a closure member configured for fluid-tight engagement in an access port of a dispenser. A valve assembly according to claim 4, wherein the septum is supported in an opening defined in said closure. A valve assembly according to claim 4 or claim 5, wherein said opening includes a receiving arrangement for receiving an end of the fluid transfer conduit, the receiving arrangement configured to locate the end of the fluid transfer conduit proximal to a reservoir of the dispenser. A valve assembly according to claim 6, wherein said receiving arrangement comprises a stopper that is configured to engage with said end of said fluid transfer conduit. 

 A valve assembly according to claim 7, wherein said stopper is an annular flange formed around an inner edge of said opening. A valve assembly according to claim 7, wherein the stopper comprises one or more inwardly projected formations formed around an inner edge of said opening. A valve assembly according to any of claims 7 to 9, wherein the receiving arrangement includes a guide for guiding movement of and end of the fluid transfer conduit into engagement therein. A valve assembly according to any of claims 7 to 10, wherein the stopper is configured to be located proximal to a reservoir of a dispenser. A valve assembly according to any preceding claim, wherein said receiving arrangement further comprises a plunger having a fluid transfer channel. A valve assembly according to claim 12, wherein fluid transfer conduit comprises a first portion formed by the plunger and a second portion formed by a fluid carrier apparatus. A valve assembly according to claim 12 or claim 13, wherein the fluid transfer conduit is configured to be urged between said petaline portions to thereby elastically distend such petaline portions responsive to an input. A valve assembly according to claim 14 further comprising a resilient member that is compressed responsive to said input. A valve assembly according to claim 15, wherein the resilient member is configured for urging the fluid transfer conduit from said second configuration in which said at least two petaline portions are open into the first configuration responsive to cessation of said input. A valve assembly according to any preceding claim, wherein said septum comprises a slit valve arrangement. A valve assembly according to any of claims 1 to 16, wherein said septum comprises a duck bill valve arrangement. A valve assembly according to any of claims 1 to 16, wherein the spetum comprises an umbrella valve arrangement. A valve assembly according to any of claims 12 to 19, wherein the fluid carrier apparatus is a syringe. A valve assembly according to any of claims 12 to 19, wherein the fluid carrier apparatus is a pipette. 22. A valve assembly as hereinbefore described and with reference to respective embodiments illustrated in the drawings. 23. A closure for a dispenser according to any preceding claim. 24. A closure as hereinbefore described and with reference to respective embodiments illustrated in the drawings. 25. A dispenser for dispensing a fluid comprising a valve assembly according to any of claims 1 to 22. 26. A dispenser for dispensing a fluid comprising a closure according to claim 23 or claim 24. 27. A dispenser as hereinbefore described and with reference to respective embodiments illustrated in the drawings.
